hypothesis: If the Force Delta metric (U∆) is incorporated as a physics-informed regularization term during fine-tuning of direct-force neural network potentials—penalizing the magnitude of disagreement between conservative and non-conservative force predictions—then the resulting models will exhibit not only reduced Force Delta values but also improved generalization accuracy on out-of-distribution structures compared to models fine-tuned without this regularization, because minimizing internal physical inconsistency during training directly constrains the model to learn a more self-consistent representation of the potential energy surface.
